headline: Chips Crack as Iran Fears Grip Wall Street
summary: Thursday handed investors a painful reminder that geopolitics and technology disruption can strike simultaneously. The Nasdaq shed 2.4% and the Dow Jones fell 1%, with semiconductor stocks absorbing the heaviest blows, caught between fears of a helium shortage triggered by the Iran conflict and a Google algorithm that threatens to upend memory demand. Meanwhile, oil surged past $107 a barrel, and Snap tumbled after Brussels opened a child safety probe.
